title: Time-since-pit and stint-reset state
summary: Use current and prior PitStop plus TyreLife/Stint progression to represent laps since last observed pit, fresh-tyre reset status, and post-stop cooldown.
rationale: PitStop is an allowed current-row feature, and next-lap pit probability depends on whether a car just stopped, is between stops, or is nearing another stop. The run explored stop-debt logic but did not fully isolate reset/cooldown behavior.
implementation_hint: Within each driver-race sequence, compute prior PitStop count, laps since last prior PitStop, current PitStop indicator, fresh-after-stop flags, TyreLife reset consistency, and interactions with Stint and RaceProgress. Use only current and previous rows, not future pit events.
expected_effect: Small to medium AUC gain with medium confidence.
risk: Current PitStop can be a strong proxy but can be misused if future stops are counted. Keep separate prior-only and including-current features so causality is explicit.
disabled_reason: Disabled because an external duplicate-detection mechanism marked this hypothesis as duplicate of an existing hypothesis.
